channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple’s hidden white noise feature may be just the productivity boost you need
item.description: As I write this, the most pleasing sound is washing over me—gentle waves ebbing and flowing onto the shore. Sadly, I’m not actually on some magnificent tropical beach. Instead, the sounds of the sea are being generated by my Mac.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Rich in cash, Japan automaker Toyota builds a city to test futuristic mobility
item.description: Woven City near Mount Fuji is where Japanese automaker Toyota plans to test everyday living with robotics, artificial intelligence and autonomous zero-emissions transportation.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rumble Suspended in Brazil Amid Free Speech Dispute - Supreme Court Justice Orders Platform's Shutdown
item.description: Brazilian Supreme Court justice Alexandre de Moraes orders Rumble to be suspended for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders, sparking a free speech debate.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SEC, DOJ investigate CrowdStrike deal with reseller Carahsoft
item.description: US feds are reportedly investigating a $32 million deal inked by CrowdStrike with a government reseller to provide cybersecurity tools for the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) — products the agency never used and said it didn’t even purchase.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Is your phone listening to everything you say? It’s complicated
item.description: Google, Instagram, Facebook and weather apps can track your location and use that data to serve you ads; here's what you can do to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why a proposed ban on lab-grown meat is getting pushback from Nebraska ranchers
item.description: The prospect of banning the sale of so-called lab-grown meat might seem like a no-brainer in Nebraska, where beef is king, but some of the proposal’s staunchest opposition has come from ranchers and farming groups who say they can compete without the government’s help. Nebraska Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This is the weirdest laptop I've ever seen and it reminds me of an often-mocked, thoroughly misunderstood Toshiba notebook
item.description: The Bapaco is a a Windows 11 laptop that's also a display and keyboard for Windows, macOS and Android.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Everything new on Max in March 2024
item.description: Here's the full list of everything new on Max in March 2025 – including new movie releases Sing Sing and Heretic.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Perplexity releases a censorship-free variant of Deepseek R1
item.description: Perplexity is releasing its model R1 1776, a version of Deepseek R1 with open model weights that has been post-trained to remove China’s censorship and provide more unbiased, accurate answers, according to Perplexity co-founder and CEO Aravind Srinivas. He wrote about the move on LinkedIn.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Quantum computing breakthrough? Microsoft says its new Majorana 1 chip shows we’re closer than ever
item.description: Microsoft on Wednesday unveiled a new chip that it said showed quantum computing is “years, not decades” away, joining Google and IBM in predicting that a fundamental change in computing technology is much closer than recently believed.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump keeps cutting election security jobs. Here’s what’s at risk
item.description: As the Trump administration continues to dismantle federal agencies, one that plays a critical role in U.S. infrastructure and election security faces an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE efforts to access private data spark sharp pushback
item.description: Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) efforts to tap into databases across the government is facing broad pushback from a variety of groups sounding the alarm over the privacy and security risks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati launches AI startup, recruits top talent from rivals
item.description: Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati has launched an AI startup, Thinking Machines Lab, escalating the fight for top talent and reshaping competition in the fast-evolving sector.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle extends support for on-prem Oracle Database 19c
item.description: As the status of an on-premises version of Oracle Database 23ai remains in limbo, the company has tossed a bone to its on-prem Oracle Database 19c customers in the form of an extension of Premier Support to Dec. 31, 2029, and Extended Support to Dec. 31, 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 ways to fix OneDrive’s worst annoyances on Windows
item.description: On Windows, OneDrive is built right into the operating system. Even if you don’t turn it on, it will pop up occasionally, asking you to “back up your files to the cloud.” What that really means, of course, is that Microsoft wants you to move your folders to OneDrive. I’m not here to knock OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dell secures deal worth more than $5B in Elon Musk's xAI startup company
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is one step closer to becoming a reality after Dell secured a deal worth more than $5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: 3 key features of Postman’s AI Agent Builder
item.description: The software landscape is shifting from passive business processes to dynamic, AI-driven workflows. AI agents—systems that interact with APIs, make decisions, and execute complex tasks—are at the forefront of this transformation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth, consumers
item.description: Since taking office, Trump has said he would place tariffs on foreign production of computer chips and semiconductors in order to return chip manufacturing to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inflatable tiny house with space for six is blowing up
item.description: An inflatable six-person tiny house from a firm called 2001 looks like a space station with its inflatable dome that is transportable and eco-friendly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
